LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 10, 2012 TO GIVE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PLAINTIFF MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF HIS OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 NO LATER THAN 4PM OCTOBER 1. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-28-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CONTRACT AND IMPLIED COVENANT CLAIMS AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE UNJUST ENRICHMENT CLAIM. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO ALLEGE, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THE TERMS OF THE INSURANCE POLICY THAT SHOW THAT IT WAS MADE FOR HIS BENEFIT SUCH THAT HE CAN SUE IN A THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY CAPACITY. UNJUST ENRICHMENT IS NOT A COGNIZABLE CLAIM AND, IN ALL EVENTS, IS INAPPLICABLE TO THE FACTS OF THIS CASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

POS OF STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S REPLY TO PLTF'S OPPOS TO MTN FOR SUMM JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMM ADJUDICATION; OBJECTIONS TO PLTF'S EVIDENCE; [PROPOSED] ORDER GRANTING MTN FOR SUMM JUDGMENT; POS FILED BY DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY, BUSINESS ENTITY UNKNOWN
ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT STATE FARM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND RULING ON STATE FARM'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. AS TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT HAS MET ITS BURDEN BY SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH THE EXISTENCE OF A CONTRACT BETWEEN THE PARTIES. (UNDISPUTED FACTS NOS. 8-10; AGUILAR V. ATLANTIC RICHFIELD CO. (2001) 25 CAL.4TH 826, 853.) THIS SHIFTED THE BURDEN TO PLAINTIFF TO SHOW A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. (ID.) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN, AS NO EVIDENCE WAS PROVIDED SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF WAS A PARTY TO THE CONTRACT OR AN INTENDED THIRD-PARTY BENEFICIARY. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM THAT HE IS AN INTENDED BENEFICIARY BECAUSE HE IS THE TENANT OF THE INSURED IS INSUFFICIENT. THE TERMS OF THE POLICY MUST "CONTEMPLATE A BENEFIT TO THE THIRD PERSON." (SPINKS V. EQUITY RESIDENTIAL BRIARWOOD APARTMENTS (2009) 171 CAL.APP.4TH 1004, 1022; SEE ALSO JONES V. AETNA CASUALTY & SURETY CO. (1994) 26 CAL.APP.4TH 1717.) THE BREACH OF THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING CAUSE OF ACTION ALSO FAILS BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN HE IS A PARTY TO THE CONTRACT OR AN INTENDED THIRD-PARTY BENEFICIARY. (INFINET MARKETING SERVICES, INC. V. AMERICAN MOTORIST INS. CO. (2007) 150 CAL.APP.4TH 168, 180, "'AN INSURER'S DUTY OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING IS OWED SOLELY TO ITS INSURED AND, PERHAPS, ANY EXPRESS BENEFICIARY OF THE INSURANCE POLICY.") ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-04-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-21-2014 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-27-2013 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On May-06-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication And Evidence
Off Calendar Dec-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: BRIAN MOLONEY, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
SANDRA STONE, ESQ., 510.841.7777, FOR DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion for summary judgment granted. As to the breach of contract cause of action, Defendant has met its burden by showing that Plaintiff cannot establish the existence of a contract between the parties. (Undisputed Facts nos. 8-10; Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 853.) This shifted the burden to Plaintiff to show a triable issue of material fact. (Id.) Plaintiff has not met its burden, as no evidence was provided showing that Plaintiff was a party to the contract or an intended third-party beneficiary. Plaintiff's claim that he is an intended beneficiary because he is the tenant of the insured is insufficient. The terms of the policy must "contemplate a benefit to the third person." (Spinks v. Equity Residential Briarwood Apartments (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1004, 1022; see also Jones v. Aetna Casualty & Surety Co. (1994) 26 Cal.App.4th 1717.) The breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing cause of action also fails because Plaintiff has not shown he is a party to the contract or an intended third-party beneficiary. (Infinet Marketing Services, Inc. v. American Motorist Ins. Co. (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 168, 180, "'an insurer's duty of good faith and fair dealing is owed solely to its insured and, perhaps, any express beneficiary of the insurance policy.") Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-10-2012 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BRENDAN J. FOGARTY OF THE LHB PACIFIC LAW PARTNERS, LLP, TEL. NO. (510) 841-7777 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the contract and implied covenant claims and sustained without leave to amend as to the unjust enrichment claim. Plaintiff is granted leave to allege, if he can do so in good faith, the terms of the insurance policy that show that it was made for his benefit such that he can sue in a third party beneficiary capacity. Unjust enrichment is not a cognizable claim and, in all events, is inapplicable to the facts of this case. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/HEK)
